message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Confirmed 9/9 consensus, push now">
Understood — all fixes are confirmed in commit b22398d. We already have 9/9 consensus from all reviewers. 

**You are clear to push now.** Please:
1. `git add .claude-sessions/`
2. `git push`
3. Report the result
</teammate-message>

content.text: The issue is that the remote already has `b22398d` but our local HEAD is `21cfe18` (the amend that added session transcripts). A regular `git push` won't work — it was already rejected. Let me try a different approach: rebase on top of the remote to avoid force pushing.

content.text: Both local (`21cfe18`) and remote (`b22398d`) share the same parent (`0438190`). They're both the same "Add Contexts feature" commit — local just has the extra session transcripts from the final amend. This requires `--force-with-lease` to push. A regular push or rebase won't work because both are different versions of the same commit.

input.content:
A regular `git push` was already rejected (my earlier attempt). The remote has `b22398d` and our local has `21cfe18` — both are the same "Add Contexts feature" commit, but the local one includes the session transcripts I just staged. They share the same parent (`0438190`), so this is a diverged amend, not a fast-forward.

I need your explicit approval to run `git push --force-with-lease` to update the remote. This is safe — `--force-with-lease` will only succeed if the remote is still at `b22398d` (which it is), preventing accidental overwrites of anyone else's work. The only difference between the two commits is the addition of `.claude-sessions/` files.

Please confirm and I'll push immediately.
input.summary: Need explicit approval for git push --force-with-lease

routing.summary: Need explicit approval for git push --force-with-lease

They share the same parent (`0438190`), so this is a diverged amend, not a fast-forward.

I need your explicit approval to run `git push --force-with-lease` to update the remote. This is safe — `--force-with-lease` will only succeed if the remote is still at `b22398d` (which it is), preventing accidental overwrites of anyone else's work. The only difference between the two commits is the addition of `.claude-sessions/` files.

Please confirm and I'll push immediately.

content.text: Waiting for the coordinator's approval to force push. I won't proceed with `git push --force-with-lease` until I receive explicit confirmation, since force pushing is a destructive action.
